steps.title: Rate of change equals slope
steps.content: The rate of change of distance with respect to time is the slope of the distance-time graph. In the equation $d=15t$, distance $d$ is the output and time $t$ is the input, so this has the same structure as $y=15x$.

steps.title: Extract the slope and apply units
steps.content: The coefficient of $t$ is $15$, so the slope is $15$. Since $d$ is measured in inches and $t$ is measured in seconds, the units are inches per second.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: The rate is the constant multiplier $15$, not the expression $15t$. B, $15$ inches per second, is correct.
